summary: The macOS CI run failed during native build steps: g++/gcc compile/link commands produced many errors while building addons and dependencies; configure was run with --error-on-warn and there are numerous deprecation warnings in deps/ncrypto.
root_cause: Compilation failed during g++/gcc build/link steps for native addons and dependencies. The job ran 'python3 ./configure --verbose --error-on-warn' and there are many warnings in '../deps/ncrypto/ncrypto.cc' which likely contributed to the build errors.

evidence.reason: Shows the job reported a large number of errors during the run.
Repeated g++ link/compile command lines indicate failure occurred during native C++ build steps.
Shows gcc was also invoked to build native objects, supporting that compilation failed.
Configure was run with --error-on-warn, which can cause warnings to be treated as errors and fail the build.
Example of deprecation/warning messages in deps/ncrypto; many such warnings were present in the log.
Indicates that addon building (native modules) was part of the build, a likely source of the compile errors.
Marks that errors were emitted to stderr during the job.

suggested_fix: Investigate and fix the native compile errors and the warnings in ../deps/ncrypto/ncrypto.cc. As a quick retry, re-run configure without --error-on-warn (remove the flag from the build) to determine whether warnings are being promoted to errors; then address the underlying deprecations or compilation failures.
